title: Observer Spotlight: M. Auma
summary: A short team highlight recognising careful review habits, reporting discipline, and the collaborative patience that strengthens Kenya asteroid campaigns.
content:
Observer spotlights are not published for volume alone. They are used when a contributor's research habits reveal something instructive about the culture of the observatory.

M. Auma's work during recent review sessions stood out for methodical checking, measured escalation of candidates, and a willingness to revisit uncertain frames rather than force conclusions. Those qualities matter deeply in asteroid work, where restraint can be as valuable as detection speed.

By documenting contributors in Dispatch, Asteonetica makes its Kenya asteroid practice more visible as a team effort rather than a set of anonymous outputs.
